While gingival hyperplasia can occur in certain subtypes like AML, it isn't a consistent feature across all forms of pediatric acute leukemia. In contrast, petechiae/purpura (option C), mucosal pallor (option B), and lymphoblastic origin (option D) are more broadly associated with childhood leukemia presentations.
